nodes.x: Why boastest thou thyself in mischief, O mighty man? the goodness of God endureth continually.

Thy powers, to find out paths to ruin, and let Out the floods of sorrow to o’erwhelm mankind, How feeble is thy sceptre 1 — how uncertain man's- Decisions drawn from thy still remaining light ! Thy beams ere long must sink in night, and Chaos reign again, hut for the power and love Of Him who gave thee; and, by revelation from His own perfected reason, shall he lead thy steps,. Till thou regain…
Why shouldest thou be as a man astonied, as a mighty man that cannot save? yet thou, O Lord, art in the midst of us, and we are called by thy name; leave us not.
Behold, God is mighty, and despiseth not any: he is mighty in strength and wisdom.
Boast not thyself of to morrow; for thou knowest not what a day may bring forth.
Say unto God, How terrible art thou in thy works! through the greatness of thy power shall thine enemies submit themselves unto thee.
Thy tongue deviseth mischiefs; like a sharp razor, working deceitfully.
Thy slanderous longue with lying mischief run, Thou lovest words that blast, devour, and burn; O that deceitful, blasting, cursed tongue! — ■
Thou givest thy mouth to evil, and thy tongue frameth deceit.
He that hath a froward heart findeth no good: and he that hath a perverse tongue falleth into mischief.
For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou choosest the tongue of the crafty.
Thou lovest evil more than good; and lying rather than to speak righteousness. Selah.
Say not thou, I will recompense evil; but wait on the Lord, and he shall save thee.
Evil shall slay the wicked: and they that hate the righteous shall be desolate.
But I say unto you, That ye resist not evil: but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.
Thou lovest all devouring words, O thou deceitful tongue.
But the word is very nigh unto thee, in thy mouth, and in thy heart, that thou mayest do it.
That thou turnest thy spirit against God, and lettest such words go out of thy mouth?
God shall likewise destroy thee for ever, he shall take thee away, and pluck thee out of thy dwelling place, and root thee out of the land of the living. Selah.
All the earth shall worship thee, and shall sing unto thee; they shall sing to thy name. Selah.
He shall send from heaven, and save me from the reproach of him that would swallow me up. Selah. God shall send forth his mercy and his truth.
Let the enemy persecute my soul, and take it; yea, let him tread down my life upon the earth, and lay mine honour in the dust. Selah.
Glorious things are spoken of thee, O city of God. Selah.

Speak unto the house of Israel, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will profane my sanctuary, the excellency of your strength, the desire of your eyes, and that which your soul pitieth; and your sons and your daughters whom ye have left shall fall by the sword.
The righteous also shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him:
The righteous see it, and are glad: and the innocent laugh them to scorn.
The Lord shall laugh at him: for he seeth that his day is coming.
He shall pray unto God, and he will be favourable unto him: and he shall see his face with joy: for he will render unto man his righteousness.
And he that liveth in righteousness shall be changed in the twinkling of an eye, and the earth shall pass away so as by fire.
And shall make him of quick understanding in the fear of the Lord: and he shall not judge after the sight of his eyes, neither reprove after the hearing of his ears:
Lo, this is the man that made not God his strength; but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.
So is he that layeth up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.
Wealth and riches shall be in his house: and his righteousness endureth for ever.
Will he esteem thy riches? no, not gold, nor all the forces of strength.

For although a man may have many revelations, and have power to do many mighty works, yet if he boasts in his own strength, and sets at naught the counsels of God, and follows after the dictates of his own will and carnal desires, he must fall and incur the vengeance of a just God upon him.

along the line of our history, as a church, we have seen them strewn by the way side, they have gone out of the church; instance after instance I could recite within my own knowledge, and you would know of a great many more than I do. This is not necessarily so. The remedy to all such cases is the same today as that which applied to the young man that came to Jesus, namely, “sell all that thou…
But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God: I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.
The Lord called thy name, A green olive tree, fair, and of goodly fruit: with the noise of a great tumult he hath kindled fire upon it, and the branches of it are broken.

Only acknowledge thine iniquity, that thou hast transgressed against the Lord thy God, and hast scattered thy ways to the strangers under every green tree, and ye have not obeyed my voice, saith the Lord.

And now, behold, notwithstanding all the care which we have taken of my vineyard, the trees thereof have become corrupted, that they bring forth no good fruit; and these I had hoped to preserve, to have laid up fruit thereof against the season, unto mine own self. But, behold, they have become like unto the wild olive tree, and they are of no worth but to be hewn down and cast into the fire; and…
When thou beatest thine olive tree, thou shalt not go over the boughs again: it shall be for the stranger, for the fatherless, and for the widow.
But I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice in thy salvation.
I will praise thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will wait on thy name; for it is good before thy saints.
I will make thy name to be remembered in all generations: therefore shall the people praise thee for ever and ever.
I will praise thee, O Lord my God, with all my heart: and I will glorify thy name for evermore.
I will freely sacrifice unto thee: I will praise thy name, O Lord; for it is good.
Now therefore, our God, we thank thee, and praise thy glorious name.
I will worship toward thy holy temple, and praise thy name for thy lovingkindness and for thy truth: for thou hast magnified thy word above all thy name.
